questions.prompt: In a row-direction flex container, align-items controls:
questions.explanation: The cross axis is vertical when direction is row.

questions.prompt: The default value of align-items is:
questions.explanation: Default is stretch — items grow to fill the cross axis.
align-items: center centers items on the cross axis.

questions.prompt: Vertically AND horizontally center the child in this container.
align-items differs from align-content in that:
options: align-items works on the whole container; align-content on each item
align-items aligns each item on the cross axis; align-content aligns MULTIPLE lines when wrapping
questions.explanation: align-content only matters when flex-wrap creates multiple lines.
Great for aligning text of different sizes on the same visual line.

questions.prompt: How to align ONE item differently from the rest?
questions.explanation: align-self overrides align-items for that specific item.

questions.prompt: What happens with align-items: stretch and no explicit heights?
questions.code:
.row { display: flex; align-items: stretch; height: 200px; }
.row > * { /* no height set */ }
questions.explanation: stretch (default) fills the cross axis when no height is set on the item.
